response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and systems software engineer with over 15 years of experience across web, mobile, and embedded domains, but his background has no meaningful overlap with the core requirements of this Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator architecture, and deep-learning workload optimization — none of which appear in his resume. His closest relevant experience is low-latency C/C++ and DLang work at Sociomantic and National Instruments, but that is far removed from GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, or silicon-level performance analysis.
strengths: Demonstrated ability to build and optimize high-throughput, low-latency systems handling millions of requests per second using async I/O and performance-aware design patterns
C and C++ experience at National Instruments writing components for an engineering IDE, showing some proximity to hardware-adjacent tooling
Staff/Principal-level track record with long tenures and broad technical scope, suggesting ability to operate independently at senior IC level
concerns: Zero experience in SoC or IP modeling, which is the primary technical deliverable of this role
No background in AI/ML, deep neural network training or inference, or any GPU/accelerator workload analysis
No SIMD, MIMD, or any hardware architecture experience — the preferred qualifications are entirely absent from the resume
